item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the writing is good, it's not much aside from a diode that started a house fire and ended in tragedy. The anomalous concept is really uninteresting, and the hidden story is pretty short and obvious.
-1; I know you can do better, OP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone know what the morse code is saying? It’s flashing too fast for me to write down in time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mind explaining what the deeper story is? It feels like the morse code thing and the total dissapearance of the father in the fire are just things that are there. I'm guessing a rather high end jolt of electricity from the LED started the fire? Maybe I'm wrong, but whatever is going on is just lost on me. Think you could post an explanation in spoiler?

Really like what you did on the blender made live feed. I don't know if I'll vote on this one, but I'd love to see that sort of creativity get used more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont think you know how electricity works.1

Footnotes
1. I am not an electric engineer but I have some experience with electronics and I dont think your depiction is quite realistic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this has the right ingredients, but got taken out of the oven too soon. There are inklings of a subtext here that should probably be more textual, and the interview could use a little more work. In particular, I dislike the overuse of ellipses to show grief; in most cases, words carry more emotional weight when punctuated with a single period. Ellipses just make her sound sleepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I've changed it to instead use the PoI format. I wanted to use that initially, but I wasn't sure, as I didn't know if PoIs were allowed to keep their designation after death or not. Thanks for the advice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've added it as a footnote, thanks for the tip!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels… okay?
I will admit that I was expecting something else from the title, but this isn't necessarily a bad idea. Here's what I think: The writing could use some polishing/feedback, and whatever 'hidden story' there is seems to well-hidden and esoteric given the number of people that know Morse Code in this day and age.

The idea seems a bit thin, and some of the wording ("the burning down of a house") is weird. Also, individuals related to an SCP usually aren't given SCP classification. SCP-XXXX-X formatting is usually reserved for sub-parts of an SCP that also have anamalous properties. See SCP-004 and SCP-072 for examples.

I'm sorry if this is rude, I just want this SCP to be good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the morse code snippet at the bottom, I'd recommend adding a translation (perhaps a "View Translation" dropdown.) Morse code translators are really easy to find and it just saves the reader a step. If the effect of translating it was intentional than by all means keep it.

And I know that LED gif is in morse code, but it's a little too fast for me to work out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to like this - the live feed gif is super cool, and I like down-to-earth stories about simple things like families dying in housefires, but there's not enough here for me to get into. I think this could have used a couple more proofreads, there were some odd word choices here and there, but that's not affecting my vote; it just feels like maybe the deeper story is too well hidden.

item.title: Author Post!
item.content:encoded:
First attempt at an SCP. I wanted to go for something that's simple on the surface, but hides a deeper story underneath.

Huge thanks to CaptainKirby for the critique, and the SCP Declassified Discord server for discussion on ideas!

"Live feed" gif was made by me in Blender, and the LED model is from here.
